quotes.text: When you're writing a pop song, you're the center of the universe. When you're scoring a film, you're just the wallpaper. But if you do it right, you're the wallpaper that makes everyone in the room feel incredibly uneasy.
quotes.speaker: Trent Reznor
quotes.fake_note: Reznor often discusses the ego-death required for film scoring compared to leading Nine Inch Nails. The giveaway is the comedic timing of the 'wallpaper' punchline, which leans slightly more self-deprecating than his usual intensely serious technical discussions.

quotes.text: I can't possibly accept this award.
quotes.speaker: Adele
quotes.context: Accepting Album of the Year for '25' at the 2017 Grammy Awards, Adele stunned the room by opening her acceptance speech declaring she couldn't accept the award, then turning to praise Beyonce — whose 'Lemonade' she felt should have won. A s

source.title: GRAMMY Rewind: Adele Urges That Beyonce's 'Monumental' 'Lemonade' Should've Won Album Of The Year In 2017

quotes.text: Nobody touches the shepherd's pie till I've been in there. Don't bust my crust, baby.
quotes.speaker: Keith Richards
quotes.context: The Rolling Stones guitarist explaining his strict on-the-road rule about his pre-show shepherd's pie, written in his autobiography 'Life'. Oddly specific and very funny rock-star demand. Innocuous: it's about food and touring rituals, noth

source.title: Don't mess with Keith Richards' pre-show shepherd's pie ritual, or he might threaten to cut you up and bake you into one

quotes.text: I don't listen for the notes. The notes are just the vehicle. I listen to see if the room changes temperature when the song is playing. If the room doesn't change, we haven't found it yet.
quotes.speaker: Rick Rubin
quotes.context: Podcast interview about his production philosophy
quotes.fake_note: Rubin constantly talks about feeling over technicality and reducing music to spiritual or energetic responses. The giveaway is the 'changes temperature' metaphor, which is a bit more literal than his usual abstract, meditative vocabulary.

quotes.text: Every time I write a song, I'm still that same girl sitting on the floor in a velvet jacket, trying to make sense of a broken heart. You never really outgrow the ghosts, you just learn how to sing with them.
quotes.speaker: Stevie Nicks
quotes.fake_note: Nicks frequently references her younger self, her signature wardrobe, and spirits/ghosts in her work. The giveaway is the final aphorism, which is a bit too neatly packaged for her typical rambling, stream-of-consciousness interview style.

quotes.text: A genius is the one most like himself.
quotes.speaker: Thelonious Monk
quotes.context: One line from 'T. Monk's Advice' — a set of pointers the jazz pianist Thelonious Monk gave saxophonist Steve Lacy, who wrote them down while playing in Monk's band in 1960. Wry and oddly specific aphorism about originality; sits alongside g

source.title: Thelonious Monk’s Advice — Howard Rees’ Jazz Workshops (transcribed by Steve Lacy, 1960)
